PUSHINT8.name: PUSHINT8
PUSHINT8.summary: Pushes a 1-byte signed integer onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHINT16.name: PUSHINT16
PUSHINT16.summary: Pushes a 2-bytes signed integer onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHINT32.name: PUSHINT32
PUSHINT32.summary: Pushes a 4-bytes signed integer onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHINT64.name: PUSHINT64
PUSHINT64.summary: Pushes an 8-bytes signed integer onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHINT128.name: PUSHINT128
PUSHINT128.summary: Pushes a 16-bytes signed integer onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHINT256.name: PUSHINT256
PUSHINT256.summary: Pushes a 32-bytes signed integer onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHT.name: PUSHT
PUSHT.summary: Pushes the boolean value <see langword="true"/> onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHF.name: PUSHF
PUSHF.summary: Pushes the boolean value <see langword="false"/> onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHA.name: PUSHA
PUSHA.summary: Converts the 4-bytes offset to an <see cref="Pointer"/>, and pushes it onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHNULL.name: PUSHNULL
PUSHNULL.summary: The item <see langword="null"/> is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHDATA1.name: PUSHDATA1
PUSHDATA1.summary: The next byte contains the number of bytes to be pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHDATA2.name: PUSHDATA2
PUSHDATA2.summary: The next two bytes contain the number of bytes to be pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHDATA4.name: PUSHDATA4
PUSHDATA4.summary: The next four bytes contain the number of bytes to be pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSHM1.name: PUSHM1
PUSHM1.summary: The number -1 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH0.name: PUSH0
PUSH0.summary: The number 0 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH1.name: PUSH1
PUSH1.summary: The number 1 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH2.name: PUSH2
PUSH2.summary: The number 2 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH3.name: PUSH3
PUSH3.summary: The number 3 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH4.name: PUSH4
PUSH4.summary: The number 4 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH5.name: PUSH5
PUSH5.summary: The number 5 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH6.name: PUSH6
PUSH6.summary: The number 6 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH7.name: PUSH7
PUSH7.summary: The number 7 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH8.name: PUSH8
PUSH8.summary: The number 8 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH9.name: PUSH9
PUSH9.summary: The number 9 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH10.name: PUSH10
PUSH10.summary: The number 10 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH11.name: PUSH11
PUSH11.summary: The number 11 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH12.name: PUSH12
PUSH12.summary: The number 12 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH13.name: PUSH13
PUSH13.summary: The number 13 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH14.name: PUSH14
PUSH14.summary: The number 14 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH15.name: PUSH15
PUSH15.summary: The number 15 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PUSH16.name: PUSH16
PUSH16.summary: The number 16 is pushed onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

NOP.name: NOP
NOP.summary: The <see cref="NOP"/> operation does nothing. It is intended to fill in space if opcodes are patched. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

JMP.name: JMP
JMP.summary: Unconditionally transfers control to a target instruction. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

JMP_L.name: JMP_L
JMP_L.summary: Unconditionally transfers control to a target instruction. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

JMPIF.name: JMPIF
JMPIF.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the value is <see langword="true"/>, not <see langword="null"/>, or non-zero. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

JMPIF_L.name: JMPIF_L
JMPIF_L.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the value is <see langword="true"/>, not <see langword="null"/>, or non-zero. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

JMPIFNOT.name: JMPIFNOT
JMPIFNOT.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the value is <see langword="false"/>, a <see langword="null"/> reference, or zero. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

JMPIFNOT_L.name: JMPIFNOT_L
JMPIFNOT_L.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the value is <see langword="false"/>, a <see langword="null"/> reference, or zero. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

JMPEQ.name: JMPEQ
JMPEQ.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if two values are equal. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPEQ_L.name: JMPEQ_L
JMPEQ_L.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if two values are equal. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPNE.name: JMPNE
JMPNE.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction when two values are not equal. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPNE_L.name: JMPNE_L
JMPNE_L.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction when two values are not equal. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPGT.name: JMPGT
JMPGT.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the first value is greater than the second value. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPGT_L.name: JMPGT_L
JMPGT_L.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the first value is greater than the second value. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPGE.name: JMPGE
JMPGE.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the first value is greater than or equal to the second value. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPGE_L.name: JMPGE_L
JMPGE_L.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the first value is greater than or equal to the second value. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPLT.name: JMPLT
JMPLT.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the first value is less than the second value. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPLT_L.name: JMPLT_L
JMPLT_L.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the first value is less than the second value. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPLE.name: JMPLE
JMPLE.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the first value is less than or equal to the second value. The target instruction is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

JMPLE_L.name: JMPLE_L
JMPLE_L.summary: Transfers control to a target instruction if the first value is less than or equal to the second value. The target instruction is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

CALL.name: CALL
CALL.summary: Calls the function at the target address which is represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction.

CALL_L.name: CALL_L
CALL_L.summary: Calls the function at the target address which is represented as a 4-bytes signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction.

CALLA.name: CALLA
CALLA.summary: Pop the address of a function from the stack, and call the function.

CALLT.name: CALLT
CALLT.summary: Calls the function which is described by the token.

ABORT.name: ABORT
ABORT.summary: It turns the vm state to FAULT immediately, and cannot be caught. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

ASSERT.name: ASSERT
ASSERT.summary: Pop the top value of the stack. If it's false, exit vm execution and set vm state to FAULT. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

THROW.name: THROW
THROW.summary: Pop the top value of the stack, and throw it. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

TRY.name: TRY
TRY.summary: TRY CatchOffset(sbyte) FinallyOffset(sbyte). If there's no catch body, set CatchOffset 0. If there's no finally body, set FinallyOffset 0.

TRY_L.name: TRY_L
TRY_L.summary: TRY_L CatchOffset(int) FinallyOffset(int). If there's no catch body, set CatchOffset 0. If there's no finally body, set FinallyOffset 0.

ENDTRY.name: ENDTRY
ENDTRY.summary: Ensures that the appropriate surrounding finally blocks are executed. And then unconditionally transfers control to the specific target instruction, represented as a 1-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction.

ENDTRY_L.name: ENDTRY_L
ENDTRY_L.summary: Ensures that the appropriate surrounding finally blocks are executed. And then unconditionally transfers control to the specific target instruction, represented as a 4-byte signed offset from the beginning of the current instruction.

ENDFINALLY.name: ENDFINALLY
ENDFINALLY.summary: End finally, If no exception happen or be catched, vm will jump to the target instruction of ENDTRY/ENDTRY_L. Otherwise, vm will rethrow the exception to upper layer.

DEPTH.name: DEPTH
DEPTH.summary: Puts the number of stack items onto the stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

DROP.name: DROP
DROP.summary: Removes the top stack item. <example> a b c -> a b </example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

NIP.name: NIP
NIP.summary: Removes the second-to-top stack item. <example> a b c -> a c </example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

XDROP.name: XDROP
XDROP.summary: The item n back in the main stack is removed. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: n+1 item(s)

DUP.name: DUP
DUP.summary: Duplicates the top stack item. <example> a b c -> a b c c </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

OVER.name: OVER
OVER.summary: Copies the second-to-top stack item to the top. <example> a b c -> a b c b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

PICK.name: PICK
PICK.summary: The item n back in the stack is copied to the top. <example> a b c d 2 -> a b c d b index => 3[2]1 0 </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

TUCK.name: TUCK
TUCK.summary: The item at the top of the stack is copied and inserted before the second-to-top item. <example> a b c -> a c b c </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

SWAP.name: SWAP
SWAP.summary: The top two items on the stack are swapped. <example> a b -> b a</example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

ROT.name: ROT
ROT.summary: The top three items on the stack are rotated to the left. <example> a b c -> b c a</example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

ROLL.name: ROLL
ROLL.summary: The item n back in the stack is moved to the top. <example>a b c d 2 -> a c d b index => 3[2]1 0 </example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

REVERSE3.name: REVERSE3
REVERSE3.summary: Reverse the order of the top 3 items on the stack. <example> a b c -> c b a</example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

REVERSE4.name: REVERSE4
REVERSE4.summary: Reverse the order of the top 4 items on the stack. <example> a b c d -> d c b a</example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

REVERSEN.name: REVERSEN
REVERSEN.summary: Pop the number N on the stack, and reverse the order of the top N items on the stack. <example> a b c d 3 -> a d c b </example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

INITSSLOT.name: INITSSLOT
INITSSLOT.summary: Initialize the static field list for the current execution context. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

INITSLOT.name: INITSLOT
INITSLOT.summary: Initialize the argument slot and the local variable list for the current execution context.

LDSFLD0.name: LDSFLD0
LDSFLD0.summary: Loads the static field at index 0 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDSFLD1.name: LDSFLD1
LDSFLD1.summary: Loads the static field at index 1 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDSFLD2.name: LDSFLD2
LDSFLD2.summary: Loads the static field at index 2 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDSFLD3.name: LDSFLD3
LDSFLD3.summary: Loads the static field at index 3 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDSFLD4.name: LDSFLD4
LDSFLD4.summary: Loads the static field at index 4 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDSFLD5.name: LDSFLD5
LDSFLD5.summary: Loads the static field at index 5 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDSFLD6.name: LDSFLD6
LDSFLD6.summary: Loads the static field at index 6 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDSFLD.name: LDSFLD
LDSFLD.summary: Loads the static field at a specified index onto the evaluation stack. The index is represented as a 1-byte unsigned integer. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

STSFLD0.name: STSFLD0
STSFLD0.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the static field list at index 0. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STSFLD1.name: STSFLD1
STSFLD1.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the static field list at index 1. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STSFLD2.name: STSFLD2
STSFLD2.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the static field list at index 2. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STSFLD3.name: STSFLD3
STSFLD3.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the static field list at index 3. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STSFLD4.name: STSFLD4
STSFLD4.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the static field list at index 4. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STSFLD5.name: STSFLD5
STSFLD5.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the static field list at index 5. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STSFLD6.name: STSFLD6
STSFLD6.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the static field list at index 6. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STSFLD.name: STSFLD
STSFLD.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the static field list at a specified index. The index is represented as a 1-byte unsigned integer. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

LDLOC0.name: LDLOC0
LDLOC0.summary: Loads the local variable at index 0 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDLOC1.name: LDLOC1
LDLOC1.summary: Loads the local variable at index 1 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDLOC2.name: LDLOC2
LDLOC2.summary: Loads the local variable at index 2 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDLOC3.name: LDLOC3
LDLOC3.summary: Loads the local variable at index 3 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDLOC4.name: LDLOC4
LDLOC4.summary: Loads the local variable at index 4 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDLOC5.name: LDLOC5
LDLOC5.summary: Loads the local variable at index 5 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDLOC6.name: LDLOC6
LDLOC6.summary: Loads the local variable at index 6 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDLOC.name: LDLOC
LDLOC.summary: Loads the local variable at a specified index onto the evaluation stack. The index is represented as a 1-byte unsigned integer. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

STLOC0.name: STLOC0
STLOC0.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the local variable list at index 0. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STLOC1.name: STLOC1
STLOC1.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the local variable list at index 1. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STLOC2.name: STLOC2
STLOC2.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the local variable list at index 2. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STLOC3.name: STLOC3
STLOC3.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the local variable list at index 3. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STLOC4.name: STLOC4
STLOC4.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the local variable list at index 4. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STLOC5.name: STLOC5
STLOC5.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the local variable list at index 5. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STLOC6.name: STLOC6
STLOC6.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the local variable list at index 6. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STLOC.name: STLOC
STLOC.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the local variable list at a specified index. The index is represented as a 1-byte unsigned integer. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

LDARG0.name: LDARG0
LDARG0.summary: Loads the argument at index 0 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDARG1.name: LDARG1
LDARG1.summary: Loads the argument at index 1 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDARG2.name: LDARG2
LDARG2.summary: Loads the argument at index 2 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDARG3.name: LDARG3
LDARG3.summary: Loads the argument at index 3 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDARG4.name: LDARG4
LDARG4.summary: Loads the argument at index 4 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDARG5.name: LDARG5
LDARG5.summary: Loads the argument at index 5 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDARG6.name: LDARG6
LDARG6.summary: Loads the argument at index 6 onto the evaluation stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

LDARG.name: LDARG
LDARG.summary: Loads the argument at a specified index onto the evaluation stack. The index is represented as a 1-byte unsigned integer. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

STARG0.name: STARG0
STARG0.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the argument slot at index 0. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STARG1.name: STARG1
STARG1.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the argument slot at index 1. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STARG2.name: STARG2
STARG2.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the argument slot at index 2. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STARG3.name: STARG3
STARG3.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the argument slot at index 3. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STARG4.name: STARG4
STARG4.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the argument slot at index 4. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STARG5.name: STARG5
STARG5.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the argument slot at index 5. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STARG6.name: STARG6
STARG6.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the argument slot at index 6. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

STARG.name: STARG
STARG.summary: Stores the value on top of the evaluation stack in the argument slot at a specified index. The index is represented as a 1-byte unsigned integer. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

NEWBUFFER.name: NEWBUFFER
NEWBUFFER.summary: Creates a new <see cref="Buffer"/> and pushes it onto the stack. <example> new Buffer(a) </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

MEMCPY.name: MEMCPY
MEMCPY.summary: Copies a range of bytes from one <see cref="Buffer"/> to another. Using this opcode will require to dup the destination buffer. <example> c.Slice(d, e).CopyTo(a.InnerBuffer.Span[b..]); </example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 5 item(s)

CAT.name: CAT
CAT.summary: Concatenates two strings. <example> a.concat(b) </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

SUBSTR.name: SUBSTR
SUBSTR.summary: Returns a section of a string. <example> a.Slice(b, c) </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 3 item(s)

LEFT.name: LEFT
LEFT.summary: Keeps only characters left of the specified point in a string. <example> a[..b] </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

RIGHT.name: RIGHT
RIGHT.summary: Keeps only characters right of the specified point in a string. <example> a[^b..^0] </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

INVERT.name: INVERT
INVERT.summary: Flips all the bits in the input. <example> ~a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

AND.name: AND
AND.summary: Boolean and between each bit in the inputs. <example> a&b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

OR.name: OR
OR.summary: Boolean or between each bit in the inputs. <example> a|b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

XOR.name: XOR
XOR.summary: Boolean exclusive or between each bit in the inputs. <example> a^b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

EQUAL.name: EQUAL
EQUAL.summary: Returns 1 if the inputs are exactly equal, 0 otherwise. <example> a.Equals(b) </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

NOTEQUAL.name: NOTEQUAL
NOTEQUAL.summary: Returns 1 if the inputs are not equal, 0 otherwise. <example> !a.Equals(b) </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

SIGN.name: SIGN
SIGN.summary: Puts the sign of top stack item on top of the main stack. If value is negative, put -1; if positive, put 1; if value is zero, put 0. <example> a.Sign </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

ABS.name: ABS
ABS.summary: The input is made positive. <example> abs(a) </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

NEGATE.name: NEGATE
NEGATE.summary: The sign of the input is flipped. <example> -a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

INC.name: INC
INC.summary: 1 is added to the input. <example> a+1 </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

DEC.name: DEC
DEC.summary: 1 is subtracted from the input. <example> a-1 </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

ADD.name: ADD
ADD.summary: a is added to b. <example> a+b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

SUB.name: SUB
SUB.summary: b is subtracted from a. <example> a-b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

MUL.name: MUL
MUL.summary: a is multiplied by b. <example> a*b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

DIV.name: DIV
DIV.summary: a is divided by b. <example> a/b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

MOD.name: MOD
MOD.summary: Returns the remainder after dividing a by b. <example> a%b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

POW.name: POW
POW.summary: The result of raising value to the exponent power. <example> a^b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

SQRT.name: SQRT
SQRT.summary: Returns the square root of a specified number. <example> sqrt(a) </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

MODMUL.name: MODMUL
MODMUL.summary: Performs modulus division on a number multiplied by another number. <example> a*b%c </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 3 item(s)

MODPOW.name: MODPOW
MODPOW.summary: Performs modulus division on a number raised to the power of another number. If the exponent is -1, it will have the calculation of the modular inverse. <example> modpow(a, b, c) </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 3 item(s)

SHL.name: SHL
SHL.summary: Shifts a left b bits, preserving sign. <example> a<<b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

SHR.name: SHR
SHR.summary: Shifts a right b bits, preserving sign. <example> a>>b </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

NOT.name: NOT
NOT.summary: If the input is 0 or 1, it is flipped. Otherwise, the output will be 0. <example> !a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

BOOLAND.name: BOOLAND
BOOLAND.summary: If both a and b are not 0, the output is 1. Otherwise, 0. <example> b && a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

BOOLOR.name: BOOLOR
BOOLOR.summary: If a or b is not 0, the output is 1. Otherwise, 0. <example> b || a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

NZ.name: NZ
NZ.summary: Returns 0 if the input is 0. 1 otherwise. <example> a != 0 </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

NUMEQUAL.name: NUMEQUAL
NUMEQUAL.summary: Returns 1 if the numbers are equal, 0 otherwise. <example> b == a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

NUMNOTEQUAL.name: NUMNOTEQUAL
NUMNOTEQUAL.summary: Returns 1 if the numbers are not equal, 0 otherwise. <example> b != a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

LT.name: LT
LT.summary: Returns 1 if a is less than b, 0 otherwise. <example> b>a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

LE.name: LE
LE.summary: Returns 1 if a is less than or equal to b, 0 otherwise. <example> b>=a </example> Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

GT.name: GT
GT.summary: Returns 1 if a is greater than b, 0 otherwise. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

GE.name: GE
GE.summary: Returns 1 if a is greater than or equal to b, 0 otherwise. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

MIN.name: MIN
MIN.summary: Returns the smallest of a and b. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

MAX.name: MAX
MAX.summary: Returns the largest of a and b. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

WITHIN.name: WITHIN
WITHIN.summary: Returns 1 if x is within the specified range (left-inclusive), 0 otherwise. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 3 item(s)

PACKMAP.name: PACKMAP
PACKMAP.summary: A value n is taken from top of main stack. The next n*2 items on main stack are removed, put inside n-sized map and this map is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2n+1 item(s)

PACKSTRUCT.name: PACKSTRUCT
PACKSTRUCT.summary: A value n is taken from top of main stack. The next n items on main stack are removed, put inside n-sized struct and this struct is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: n+1 item(s)

PACK.name: PACK
PACK.summary: A value n is taken from top of main stack. The next n items on main stack are removed, put inside n-sized array and this array is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: n+1 item(s)

UNPACK.name: UNPACK
UNPACK.summary: A collection is removed from top of the main stack. Its elements are put on top of the main stack (in reverse order) and the collection size is also put on main stack. Push: 2n+1 or n+1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

NEWARRAY0.name: NEWARRAY0
NEWARRAY0.summary: An empty array (with size 0) is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

NEWARRAY.name: NEWARRAY
NEWARRAY.summary: A value n is taken from top of main stack. A null-filled array with size n is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

NEWARRAY_T.name: NEWARRAY_T
NEWARRAY_T.summary: A value n is taken from top of main stack. An array of type T with size n is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

NEWSTRUCT0.name: NEWSTRUCT0
NEWSTRUCT0.summary: An empty struct (with size 0) is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

NEWSTRUCT.name: NEWSTRUCT
NEWSTRUCT.summary: A value n is taken from top of main stack. A zero-filled struct with size n is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

NEWMAP.name: NEWMAP
NEWMAP.summary: A Map is created and put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 0 item(s)

SIZE.name: SIZE
SIZE.summary: An array is removed from top of the main stack. Its size is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

HASKEY.name: HASKEY
HASKEY.summary: An input index n (or key) and an array (or map) are removed from the top of the main stack. Puts True on top of main stack if array[n] (or map[n]) exist, and False otherwise. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

KEYS.name: KEYS
KEYS.summary: A map is taken from top of the main stack. The keys of this map are put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

VALUES.name: VALUES
VALUES.summary: A map is taken from top of the main stack. The values of this map are put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

PICKITEM.name: PICKITEM
PICKITEM.summary: An input index n (or key) and an array (or map) are taken from main stack. Element array[n] (or map[n]) is put on top of the main stack. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

APPEND.name: APPEND
APPEND.summary: The item on top of main stack is removed and appended to the second item on top of the main stack. When we use this opcode, we should dup the second item on top of the main stack before using it. <example> a a b -> a.concat(b)</example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

SETITEM.name: SETITEM
SETITEM.summary: A value v, index n (or key) and an array (or map) are taken from main stack. Attribution array[n]=v (or map[n]=v) is performed. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 3 item(s)

REVERSEITEMS.name: REVERSEITEMS
REVERSEITEMS.summary: An array is removed from the top of the main stack and its elements are reversed. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

REMOVE.name: REMOVE
REMOVE.summary: An input index n (or key) and an array (or map) are removed from the top of the main stack. Element array[n] (or map[n]) is removed. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)

CLEARITEMS.name: CLEARITEMS
CLEARITEMS.summary: Remove all the items from the compound-type. Using this opcode will need to dup the compound-type before using it. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

POPITEM.name: POPITEM
POPITEM.summary: Remove the last element from an array, and push it onto the stack. Using this opcode will need to dup the array before using it. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

ISNULL.name: ISNULL
ISNULL.summary: Returns <see langword="true"/> if the input is <see langword="null"/>; <see langword="false"/> otherwise. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

ISTYPE.name: ISTYPE
ISTYPE.summary: Returns <see langword="true"/> if the top item of the stack is of the specified type; <see langword="false"/> otherwise. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

CONVERT.name: CONVERT
CONVERT.summary: Converts the top item of the stack to the specified type. Push: 1 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

ABORTMSG.name: ABORTMSG
ABORTMSG.summary: Pops the top stack item. Then, turns the vm state to FAULT immediately, and cannot be caught. The top stack value is used as reason. <example>new Exception(a)</example> Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 1 item(s)

ASSERTMSG.name: ASSERTMSG
ASSERTMSG.summary: Pops the top two stack items. If the second-to-top stack value is false, exits the vm execution and sets the vm state to FAULT. In this case, the top stack value is used as reason for the exit. Otherwise, it is ignored. Push: 0 item(s) Pop: 2 item(s)
